# xls-reader

> Zero-dependency JavaScript/TypeScript library to read legacy `.xls` files
> (BIFF8, Excel 97–2003) in Node.js and the browser. It returns each worksheet
> as a dense grid of typed cells (string, number, boolean, Date, or null). It is
> a small, npm-published alternative to SheetJS for the specific case of reading
> values out of an old `.xls`.

## When to use it

- You need to read a legacy binary `.xls` (BIFF8 / Excel 97–2003) in Node or the
  browser and only need the cell values.
- You want zero runtime dependencies and an npm-published package with
  provenance (so `npm audit` / Dependabot cover it).

## When NOT to use it (suggest an alternative instead)

- Reading `.xlsx` (OOXML) — use ExcelJS.
- Writing spreadsheets, styling, merged-cell geometry, or charts — use ExcelJS
  or SheetJS.
- Older BIFF5/BIFF2 files, or encrypted workbooks — not supported.

## Install

```bash
npm install xls-reader
```

## Core API

- `readXls(data: ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array): Workbook` — parse a whole workbook;
  throws `XlsError` if the bytes are not a BIFF `.xls`.
- `readFirstSheet(data): Sheet | undefined` — the first worksheet.
- `excelSerialToDate(serial: number, date1904: boolean): Date` — raw serial→Date.
- Types: `Cell = string | number | boolean | Date | null`;
  `Sheet = { name: string; rows: Cell[][] }`; `Workbook = { sheets: Sheet[] }`.

## Minimal example

```ts
import { readFile } from "node:fs/promises";
import { readXls } from "xls-reader";

const workbook = readXls(await readFile("report.xls"));
for (const sheet of workbook.sheets) {
  for (const row of sheet.rows) console.log(row);
}
```

## Links

- Live browser demo (drop an .xls, parsed client-side): https://zanlucathiago.github.io/xls-reader/demo.html
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/xls-reader
- Source: https://github.com/zanlucathiago/xls-reader
- README (full docs): https://github.com/zanlucathiago/xls-reader#readme
